metadata.title: Literature Leads Are Not Citations
metadata.description_blurb: Claude names real scholars in plausible-sounding citations that do not exist. Treating a literature map as a bibliography is a credibility error — the verification step is not optional.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: The grant application cited four scholars. Claude named real people. Real journals. Plausible titles. None of the four articles could be found in the library database. Two of the scholars had never published in that journal. One article title did not exist under that author's name anywhere. The faculty member submitted a grant that cited work Claude invented. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, sketch literature map for grant application

props.command: sketch the major conceptual debates in adaptive feedback practices in first-year writing

beats.narration_text: Claude does not fabricate because it intends to mislead. It generates text that is statistically coherent with its training. When it encounters a gap between what it knows and what the prompt requires, it produces plausible-sounding text to fill it. Real scholars. Real journals. Assembled into citations that were never published. The output reads as authoritative. The content may be invented.
beats.new_visual_element: Hallucination mechanism — statistical coherence fills gap with plausible-sounding citation

beats.narration_text: The valid use is lead generation. Claude can sketch the terrain: name likely conceptual debates, identify scholars associated with positions, suggest journals that publish in the area, generate search terms. That output is a map. It tells you where to look. It does not tell you what is there. Every scholar name, journal name, and conceptual claim is a hypothesis until you verify it in a database.
beats.new_visual_element: Lead generation map — conceptual terrain / scholars to check / search terms to try

beats.narration_text: The verification step is not optional. For every scholar name Claude produces: go to your library database and confirm they have published what Claude describes. For every title: confirm it exists under that author's name. For every journal: confirm the article appears in that issue. This is not distrust of Claude — it is the minimum due diligence for scholarly work. A grant claim that misstates the literature damages credibility that took years to build.
beats.new_visual_element: Verification checklist — scholar / title / journal must each be confirmed in database

beats.narration_text: The discipline is one word in the frame you give Claude: leads, not citations. That framing is for you, not Claude — it changes how you use the output. Claude's literature map is the same whether you frame it as leads or citations. What changes is whether you treat every scholar name as a verified source or an unverified hypothesis to check. The verification step is the difference between tool use and a credibility error.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — frame: leads not citations / verification is the line between tool use and error
beats.spark_line: Frame it leads. Verify before citing.

beats.narration_text: Use this prompt for your next literature exploration. It frames the output as leads from the start.

props.command: I am beginning research on [topic]. Sketch the major conceptual debates, name scholars associated with each position, suggest journals most likely to publish in this area, and suggest database search terms. Flag anything you are less confident about. I will treat every name and title as an unverified lead until I confirm it in my library database — do not present any citation as verified.

beats.narration_text: If this stops an invented citation from reaching a grant application, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeTitleOutro — Literature Leads Are Not Citations.
